title: Undercut/Overcut Duel Features
summary: Official F1 strategy analysis says undercut/overcut success depends on being close to rivals, tyre warm-up, degradation, and clean air.
rationale: Official F1 strategy analysis says undercut/overcut success depends on being close to rivals, tyre warm-up, degradation, and clean air. The current best code is rich in tyre-state features but still light on explicit rival-specific pit-window interactions.
implementation_hint: Create ahead/behind duel features inside each race-lap: local position neighbors, same/other-compound flags, relative tyre life/degradation percentiles, local lap-delta percentile, and proxies for undercut-favorable vs overcut-favorable conditions. Add interactions with compound class and race phase.
expected_effect: Should capture the tactical trigger for next-lap stops better than global field averages alone, especially around midfield cover-offs.
risk: No explicit time-gap column exists, so duel pressure must be approximated from available ordering and pace features; weak proxies could add noise.
